Uncle Lit 2025-03-29 6:32 p.m.

Uncle Lit 2025-03-29 6:54 p.m.
putting this here for future reference: I can now reliably reconnect a device via bluetooth on the pi, but the steps are super jank.
  • delete device from the pi desktop, bluetoothctl remove doesn't work
  • reboot Heltec V3, remember the new PIN
  • run bluttoothctl, scan on, then pair using mac address
  • after successful pair, turn off and on the heltec v3
  • when it is back on, script using bluetooth should be able to send messages
Glen (NZ) 2025-03-29 8:49 p.m.

Glen (NZ)
I'm still trying to figure out how the handle_rx method in the MeshCore class relates to the handle_rx in Serial and TCP, whereas BLE appears to just use the MeshCore class's handle_rx, it doesn't have one of its own? (edited)
FDLamotte 2025-03-30 2:12 a.m.
there is a difference because, in TCP and Serial, commands are put into a frame, which len can be greater or lower than the command len ... but in the end handle_rx is called https://github.com/fdlamotte/meshcore_py/blob/7e39b660491309a35ad22cace4c1eca9f6ca160b/src/meshcore/mclib.py#L85-L86
1
1
2:13 a.m.
while handle_rx in the connection receives frames, handle_rx in mc receives commands

awol 2025-04-08 1:19 p.m.
ct is usually the node name
1:26 p.m.
I wanted to share some thoughts on our current CLI implementation for the mesh network communication, particularly as I'm running into limitations when integrating with Home Assistant. The single-threaded nature of our CLI interaction is becoming problematic. Here's what I'm seeing:
  • During long-running operations (like status updates or logins), we're not processing incoming messages because of how the underlying meshcore_py library works
  • When using operations like login -> wait_login, any messages received while waiting for a specific response are acknowledged but never processed
  • Messages are marked as "read" even if they arrive during wait windows since they're received by the serial connection
This is especially problematic with Home Assistant integration, which requires a more continuous processing model rather than the discrete, command-by-command approach the CLI was designed for. HA needs to maintain a long-running connection where messages are processed continuously, not just when we're explicitly waiting for them. I'm thinking of implementing these improvements: 1. Create an internal message queue to guarantee all messages get processed 2. Redesign the underlying architecture to:
  • Maintain open connections
  • Feed all incoming packets into an eventing/fanout system
  • Allow message sending via commands without blocking
  • Let consumers wait for events without holding connections open
The current implementation is fine for CLI usage where operations are discrete and user-driven, but for integration with systems like Home Assistant that require continuous background processing, we need a more robust approach. I believe we can implement this without breaking existing CLI functionality. Looking at the meshcore_py library, I might contribute some changes to add a proper pub-sub system, which opens up a ton of other functionality opportunities with the underlying library Let me know your thoughts on this approach.
1
1
1
1
1:27 p.m.
I believe the MT library also works this way. Happy to take a stab at making this change/refactor (when I find the time)
1:28 p.m.
It also has some huge processing time benefits cause after firing a login packet to a remote node there is no reason something like HA can't fire additional commands while we wait for the login ack to return. It's basically a high latency async I/O system (edited)
Mike / N2QDK 2025-04-08 1:38 p.m.
in my case, change_path 'Thacher Repeater 1' '32' is what fixed the path in Home Assistant
1:38 p.m.
still not able to log in/query info/stats, but at least in the attributes for the node, the out path is changed/fixed (edited)
FDLamotte 2025-04-08 1:40 p.m.
I am totally ok with that ...
1:41 p.m.
but also the lib has already non-blocking ways of waiting acks, msgs
1:42 p.m.
I use semaphore for messages, futures for replies and events for acks
FDLamotte 2025-04-08 1:49 p.m.
If we end up with something better built as an evolution of my proposal, I'll be very proud of my contrib ... to be honnest, I did this to play with my mc nodes but as an electrical engineer, I lack some cs background to really do good and scalable software ...
2
1:50 p.m.
mixing the approaches (and backgrounds) seems very good to me and that's something I find good in this community
1
awol 2025-04-08 2:03 p.m.
It is true that some stuff can be done async, but the problem comes in with dispatching. if I call wait_login without also having wait_msg called I will miss any packets/frames that come in during that period. There basically needs to be a permanent and persistent listener that then routes the incoming packets to the appropriate listeners. That way wait_login and wait_msg can both behave exactly as they currently do but they rely on a lower level listener/dispatcher that serves a permenant event router (edited)
2:04 p.m.
I honestly don't think it's that big of refactor on things, most of the handle_rx code would stay exactly the same, the signatures for all the existing methods could stay the same, the abstraction can be added between the existing layers
2:08 p.m.
I will take a stab at adding the layer, and see how people feel about it
FDLamotte 2025-04-08 2:09 p.m.
great, I'll learn a lot with this one thanks for taking a look at it

in my case, change_path 'Thacher Repeater 1' '32' is what fixed the path in Home Assistant
awol 2025-04-08 2:16 p.m.

Hi all, we're moving the API to v2, as proposed by @awol ... v2 is a mix sync/evt api, the sync API is very close to v1 so transition should be easy. Evt based API will enable lots of use-cases

  • chat command
  • synthetic output
  • json output (can be set globally with -j or at each command prefixing them with a dot)

3:18 a.m.
I'm really proud of chat command, as it is also an interactive mode, where you can issue commands starting the line with a $ (edited)
FDLamotte 2025-04-17 5:03 a.m.
expect breaking changes, as I'm going to comply with terminal chat and repeater's cli (some commands are already supported)
5:04 a.m.
I'm going to clean things up, there are too much duplicates ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-17 7:46 a.m.
json output goes through jq (as long as there are no errors ;))
FDLamotte 2025-04-17 10:03 a.m.
2
10:03 a.m.
new command list ...
10:04 a.m.
I forgot to put the chat mode !
FDLamotte 2025-04-17 10:39 a.m.
chat is becoming usable, as incoming messages are now printed above current line !
10:44 a.m.
but it has issues with multiline messages
10:45 a.m.
I'll need to insert the good number of lines depending of the width of terminal and size of string ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-17 11:27 a.m.
now works with multiline ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-17 11:55 a.m.

FDLamotte 2025-04-17 12:10 p.m.
after all login works
FDLamotte 2025-04-17 12:50 p.m.

FDLamotte 2025-04-18 2:45 a.m.
updated the readme, with new commands and chat session examples https://github.com/fdlamotte/meshcore-cli/blob/main/README.md (edited)
meshcore-cli/README.md at main · fdlamotte/meshcore-cli
Command line interface to MeshCore node. Contribute to fdlamotte/meshcore-cli development by creating an account on GitHub.
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 2:55 a.m.
I'll probably tag a 1.0 soon, I think current version should be 1.0.0a ... as I have more or less the features I wanted when starting the project
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 3:20 a.m.
Before that, I'll implement cmd; login and logout for the chat (that uses the current recipient)
3:21 a.m.
also I'm starting to prefer IM instead of chat, because IM can stand for "Instant Message" but also "Interactive Mode"
Uncle Lit 2025-04-18 4:53 a.m.
I am traveling. I won't be able to play with your new release until mid May.
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 5:00 a.m.
too bad : but I hope v1 will be complete by then ...
5:01 a.m.
I try to use it and see what I miss ...
5:01 a.m.
for instance, the last thing I found is that msgs from room server should display the name of the emiter
5:01 a.m.
not difficult but another special case for my message printer
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 7:02 a.m.
meshcli will now default to chat when invoqued with no args ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 7:18 a.m.
and if invoqued with a contact name, it will open the chat to that contact
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 10:30 a.m.
I've just put the interactive mode to another level ... completion (for commands and contact names) and history !
10:30 a.m.
bumped version to 0.6, and slowly tracking bugs ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 11:22 a.m.
another video for today, showing interaction with room servers, auto-completion ...
1
1
11:24 a.m.
FDLamotte 2025-04-18 4:30 p.m.
some coloring
FDLamotte 2025-04-19 9:55 a.m.
some color tests ... different colors depending on node type (repeater, room, chat), and change text color wether it is a message or a command response (edited)
9:58 a.m.
and I treat rooms as well
10:00 a.m.
and I can also ask for json display
10:01 a.m.
and turn color off
10:07 a.m.
everything is in now 0.6.6 from pipx
10:08 a.m.
I think that's ok with features ... I'll correct bugs now and go back to techo screen
FDLamotte 2025-04-19 1:06 p.m.
well i did it again, can't help it ... fancy prompt (to distinguish better answers and replies)
1
1:07 p.m.
the color of the prompt matches the type of node ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-19 3:19 p.m.
not ack
6Toes_PlanetApe 2025-04-19 3:49 p.m.
Doing a good job
FDLamotte 2025-04-19 4:09 p.m.
mostly pleasing myself ... awol work on the lib opened a lot of possibilities ... we have a powerfull tool at hand
4:11 p.m.
now some things can be customized, for those who dislike invert printing
4:11 p.m.
and for those who read json natively
4:12 p.m.
I won't go as far as letting change the colors ... the idea is to be able to support terminals that won't work well with inverse and colors
4:13 p.m.
and to be able to use the json data in scripts
4:14 p.m.
all these settings are accessible from the completion
FDLamotte 2025-04-23 5:50 a.m.
yesterday I was confronted to a 2 weeks old install of meshcli ...
5:50 a.m.
the improvement is huge !
2
FDLamotte 2025-04-24 5:59 a.m.
I've made an update to solve issues (I try not to add functionalities)
  • added the possibility to select public (or any chanel) as recipient, with to public or to_public (because a node could be named public and would by prioritary)
  • if no contact in list, will default to public (solves an issue when the chat had no recipient, now at least there is a channel)
  • if recipient is a Room or a Repeater, messages will be sent as commands (you have to explicitely use send or prefix with " to send a message to a room server, I had too many messages sent by error and they went to everyone ... not good)
6:04 a.m.
I continue to let it stabilize, and aim to 1.0, then I'll start a big refactoring of the cmd mechanism ... the code is becoming unmanageable !
FDLamotte 2025-04-24 8:14 a.m.
this should be the last addition, to empty the open issues list
8:15 a.m.
I was already depending on prompt-toolkit for the prompt, so using other functionalities from this toolkit won't hurt ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-24 10:18 a.m.
I think v1 will be for next week ... I won't hurry because I've made some big changes today and they need to be proven but it really feels good ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-24 3:21 p.m.
I have reports that it works on windows without wsl ... pipx manages to fetch all the deps ...
3:22 p.m.
the only issue is with the prompt ... but one can do set classic_prompt on that uses only ascii characters
FDLamotte 2025-04-25 3:47 a.m.
from some issue raised by fishingdev (are you fish ?)
3:48 a.m.
this can be set in the init file
FDLamotte 2025-04-25 7:23 a.m.
I still fix lots of small bugs, this morning it was on json output, I had added too much things without evaluating the effect on the json ... it should be usable now
7:27 a.m.
7:27 a.m.
I now have some users, both of my repeaters/rooms and meshcli
7:27 a.m.
I mean in the building/area
FDLamotte 2025-04-25 10:27 a.m.
in latest pull I've set public as default recipient ... before that, recipient was set to the oldest node, but it was not consistent accross devices this is not a better choice, just a default choice you can still set initial recipient on the commandline (just write its name, you don't even have to write to if the name is not also a command)
FDLamotte 2025-04-25 10:49 a.m.
some colleague is proposing to have some sort of root, on which you would not send message to any room, but just process meshcli commands, and then enter in a recipient and have commands sent in the context of that recipient ...
10:50 a.m.
that's interesting but I keep the idea for a v2, because the v1 becomes too bloated and I need to modularize things a bit and do a real software architecture
FDLamotte 2025-04-25 10:58 a.m.
it's true that I sometimes want to send command and they go to the channels as messages instead ... it happens often
11:02 a.m.
or I might release v1 and start working on v2 ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-26 2:52 a.m.
well in the end ... I'll start v0.7, with some big changes in IM commands, the idea is to have the behaviour fixed for v1 and only work in inner working in v2
2:52 a.m.
it should also solve some issues in prompt when devices are added/removed
FDLamotte 2025-04-26 12:28 p.m.
just implemented a root ... where commands are direct meshcli commands ... then one enters in a node using to, leaves using to .. or to / or to ~ (edited)
12:29 p.m.
I thought I'd have to rewrite a lot of code, but in the end only some modifications were necessary ... I still need to get the completion right and I'd like to implement an help
12:30 p.m.
but the road to v1 will not be much more longer ...
12:33 p.m.
here I show what happens ... when doing clock at root, it gives the nodes time ... I then entered EnsibsRoom (clock did not succeed because it was out of reach, but command was sent to the distant node) on FdlRoom, which was joinable, I could get a response then go back with to ~ feels a little like bluetoothctl
12:34 p.m.
If I do the same to a chat node (flo2) it sends a message ... here flo2 is offline so I didn't get an ack
FDLamotte 2025-04-26 1:55 p.m.
uploaded 0.7.0 to PyPi, should be available
FDLamotte 2025-04-26 2:13 p.m.
it might quickly become v1 ...
FDLamotte 2025-04-27 7:27 a.m.
I'm going to remove wait_login and wait_status ... no real interest now, will do everything in login and get_status !
7:30 a.m.
also I have the idea of prefixing commands with _ to mute them that will be interesting for simplifying json output
FDLamotte 2025-04-27 8:33 a.m.
completion list for get/set on repeaters and rooms
1
8:36 a.m.
and some hints when setting radio
FDLamotte 2025-04-28 3:32 p.m.
I have not touched meshcore-cli today ! it seems to stabilize ...
3:33 p.m.
I have two ideas :
  • remove the array output in json mode, as it is better to pipe to jq -s finally (and get the possibility to handle each message separately)
  • implement to ! that would set recipient to the latest node we received a msg from ! so we can respond quickly ... this could be great !
 (edited)
3:39 p.m.
and there is an issue with node names with spaces, their use is not consistent ...
  • they must be used without quotes in to command
  • they must be used with quotes when used as arguments in meshcli ...
don't really know how to deal with it, and adding quotes in the completion will not be easy
FDLamotte 2025-04-28 3:52 p.m.
hehe
3:52 p.m.
that one will be great !
FDLamotte
that one will be great !
6Toes_PlanetApe 2025-04-28 4:05 p.m.
Enjoying your progress, wonderfull when ideas come to life
1
FDLamotte 2025-04-29 6:15 a.m.
small additions made this morning,
  • lines starting with ! wil send replies (msg to last sender)
  • to .. selects the previous recipient
  • possibility to use quotes around contact name in the to command
FDLamotte 2025-04-29 1:30 p.m.
there was also a big work (lots of small bugs) on correctly handling msg subscription when outputing json ...
1:31 p.m.
now you can do mccli -j msgs_subscribe wait_keyboard | jq .text and it will print you the text of incoming message as they come (just an example)
1:32 p.m.
or in condensed form mccli -j ms wk | jq .text
FDLamotte 2025-04-30 12:16 p.m.
12:16 p.m.
that is the charge of a solar node
12:17 p.m.
get the values with this command every 10 min res=$(meshcore-cli -s /dev/ttyS0 -j req_status RakSolar_fdl | jq .bat)

this morning from the phone ...

just tagged v1.0.0rc1 !

telemetry is there
3:41 p.m.

FDLamotte 2025-05-22 11:31 a.m.

one thing I see is if the client I want to message has one or mroe spaces in the name, even if I put quotes in front and after the name, it doesn't work. for example; I can message to "UncleLit" meshcore-cli -s /dev/ttyACM0 msg "UncleLit" "test" I can't message "Uncle Lit" meshcore-cli -s /dev/ttyACM0 msg "Uncle Lit" "test" (edited)

path command or contact_info
2:23 a.m.
normally with quote it should work with spaces ...

12:01 p.m.
you can do contact_info "contact" or path "contact" for a long time, I think I implemented both when I started working on the chat version, to give a synthetic info on one node (before I was only doing contact_list
FDLamotte
you can do contact_info "contact" or path "contact" for a long time, I think I implemented both when I started working on the chat version, to give a synthetic info on one node (before I was only doing contact_list
Uncle Lit 2025-06-09 12:56 p.m.

as always, its good to have users that report issues

Uncle Lit 2025-06-16 11:16 a.m.
Thanks for the update! Will test it out later today
Uncle Lit 2025-06-21 5:27 p.m.
@FDLamotte is there a way to set the path to a contact with more than one repeater? I tried a few different ways and infos always shows just one repeater
Uncle Lit 2025-06-21 5:37 p.m.
ah, I figured it out. for more than one repeater in path, just do, e.g., 75ec, no comma
1
FDLamotte 2025-06-22 2:29 a.m.
yes, it's the raw hex string
Uncle Lit 2025-07-09 3:22 p.m.
@FDLamotte in meshcore-cli, if you don't explicitly set a path to a companion, does meshcore-cli always send direct messages via flood, even if a known path is returned?
FDLamotte 2025-07-09 4:12 p.m.
no, as soon as a path is set it uses it ... it's defined in the companion radio firmware
4:12 p.m.
but you can reset it just after
FDLamotte
no, as soon as a path is set it uses it ... it's defined in the companion radio firmware
Uncle Lit 2025-07-09 4:20 p.m.
So meshcore-cli will also reset the path if existing path fails enough times?
4:24 p.m.
I guess I am asking is there a way to always send dm using flood
FDLamotte 2025-07-09 4:27 p.m.
no it does not automatically reset the path
4:28 p.m.
you'll have to reset path before sending msg
4:28 p.m.
the issue is that in current implementation, reset_path asks for contact list (to update contact)
4:28 p.m.
so it will be sub optimal
4:34 p.m.
but I think I should change that
Uncle Lit 2025-07-09 4:42 p.m.
Thanks. One test scenario is I want to always flood send dm to a target to test repeater coverage so I don't want a path be auto set once a path is found
awol 2025-07-09 6:46 p.m.
I've found with the HA integration I have to manually set the path to distant repeater I want to monitor
6:46 p.m.
otherwise they completely fail to connect
FDLamotte 2025-07-10 11:31 a.m.
@awol I just had a hard time debugging the serial connexion for meshcore_py, in the end it is connected after a timeout but it was then necessary to send a appstart ... I've moved the delay between serial port opening and mc connexion and it works again
FDLamotte 2025-07-11 4:09 a.m.
@awol I'm starting to rework contact management in meshcore_cli, and want to introduce some changes in meshcore_py for that. Can you have a look at this branch : https://github.com/fdlamotte/meshcore_py/tree/contact_mgmt and tell me what you think ? Notably, I've added a flag to tell if contact list is up to date (contacts_ok) and ensure_contacts will reload them if there was an advert or path change, but I'm not sure it won't produce a high overhead ... maybe we could just keep this flag and let the user refresh contact when he feels it's necessary ?
FDLamotte 2025-07-11 6:10 a.m.
I'm not really happy with that ... I will probably add an optional parameter (set to false) to ensure_contacts
FDLamotte 2025-07-11 9:14 a.m.
working on contact management
9:15 a.m.
you can now set manual_add_contact and see contacts coming
9:15 a.m.
you'll soon be able to add the ones you want
Uncle Lit
Thanks. One test scenario is I want to always flood send dm to a target to test repeater coverage so I don't want a path be auto set once a path is found
FDLamotte 2025-07-11 11:03 a.m.
In latest git you can now get adverts and path change as they come using set print_adverts on and set print_path_updates on I've also reworked most of the contact management not to ask the node at each change, so reset_path won't force download the list, so you can do reset_path just before msg and it will send it flood ...
11:03 a.m.
you'll need latest git for both meshcore_py and meshcore-cli
11:04 a.m.
I'll probably make a v1.1 of meshcore-cli when I've tested the new thing enough
FDLamotte
@awol I'm starting to rework contact management in meshcore_cli, and want to introduce some changes in meshcore_py for that. Can you have a look at this branch : https://github.com/fdlamotte/meshcore_py/tree/contact_mgmt and tell me what you think ? Notably, I've added a flag to tell if contact list is up to date (contacts_ok) and ensure_contacts will reload them if there was an advert or path change, but I'm not sure it won't produce a high overhead ... maybe we could just keep this flag and let the user refresh contact when he feels it's necessary ?
awol 2025-07-11 12:15 p.m.
I can take a look in a bit, on-call at work today
FDLamotte 2025-07-11 12:31 p.m.
I've finally merge in main, because I discovered some bugs I didn't want to mess with again
12:32 p.m.
but it would be cool if you can have a look, there were some changes and I'd like not to break anything ... thanks !
FDLamotte 2025-07-13 7:21 a.m.
I'm impressed how well ble connexion works now, especially on a laptop on which cx was impossible
2

FDLamotte 2025-07-16 5:02 p.m.
I've just started using lastmod in ensure_contacts ... it speeds up contact updates a lot
5:02 p.m.
at least over ble (edited)
FDLamotte 2025-07-17 2:01 a.m.
and now contacts can be automatically updated with the auto_update_contacts property @awol maybe a quick check would be good
Nickel 2025-07-17 1:22 p.m.
Im a caveman over here. I have my device plugged into my PC and im trying to use the Serial to Connect to it in Python (becasue Im stuck Remote Destkoping in and can't look at screen. any advice?
1:25 p.m.
i know i should use the S command but How do i know what port im in?
FDLamotte 2025-07-17 2:48 p.m.
-S opens the ble device selector
1
2:48 p.m.
the option you seek is -s
2:48 p.m.
and the device depends on your platform
2:49 p.m.
on Linux it is usually /dev/ttyUSB0 or /dev/ttyACM0
2:49 p.m.
also make sure you use the good companion radio firmware
2:50 p.m.
there are specific companion radios for serial connexion
2:52 p.m.

FDLamotte 2025-07-17 4:15 p.m.
I've just used mccli on termux ...
4:16 p.m.
on the android phone, which has the only purpose to run the meshcore application
4:16 p.m.
serial does not work
4:16 p.m.
nor does ble
4:17 p.m.
but you can use TCP
4:18 p.m.
and I've used a program named tcpuart to bridge an uart to tcp so you don't even need a tcp companion, just use the usb port (to power and communicate with the node)
4:19 p.m.
on mccli as TCP protocol is the same as serial one, it works transparently ...
4:19 p.m.
I was confident it was possible but had never tested ...
FDLamotte
thanks, I usually test with a pi and have already seen that kind of issue, but I missed this one this time ...

just added the possibility to have an init script for any given device in mccli (edited)

2:20 p.m.

ded (SalishMesh) 2025-07-26 9:18 p.m.
I should update, is there some kind of message history stored? I notice when staying connected for a long time that the messages scroll out of the terminal window but you cannot scroll back through them
FDLamotte 2025-07-27 1:38 a.m.
No message history, but I'm puzzled, do you have issues with the scroll back buffer of your terminal ? When I started using the prompt library scroll back was impossible with the wheel so I deactivated mouse support ... now there should be no problem scrolling back
ded (SalishMesh) 2025-07-27 1:39 a.m.
I can scroll back but last I tried, beyond the visible chat history in the terminal, there was only previous commands and not more history. I will test again and let you know
FDLamotte 2025-07-27 2:17 a.m.
oh I think it might be because I insert lines before current line ... it must eat the ones at the top of the terminal window !

FDLamotte 2025-07-28 9:17 a.m.

FDLamotte 2025-08-07 6:48 a.m.
ok, I know what happens, set_disconnect_callback was deprecated, we'll find another way

6:49 a.m.
I wanted to get rid of it anyway (it now should only be used on macs ...)
FDLamotte 2025-08-07 12:57 p.m.
Ok, I'm back home now @VP passing a client to the cx constructor implies the use of set_disconnected_callback I think it should work by passing a device (and I have already add the parameter) then the disconnected callback can be passed as a parameter when creating the client class, does that sound ok to you or in your experience, was it mandatory to pass a client ? @recrof passing the full address currently triggers the use of the "client" path but meshcore cli should work by giving the name of your node meshcore-cli -a recrof-t1000 in your case should work. It should also work with meshcore-cli -S, if so I'll just scan for the devices when I have an uuid if you could tell me how it behaves I would be sure it does what I think ...
FDLamotte 2025-08-07 1:28 p.m.
@recrof @VP @awol you can also try new mccli/mcpy, now if a BleakClient is passed (without the disconnected callback) I create a new one from the address inside ... this should resolve the issue (passing a device means doing a scan, so it's not an alternative)
VP 2025-08-07 2:46 p.m.
Weird I used the Bluetooth connection for my T 1000 and it worked
2:46 p.m.
We can absolutely port that to something that works across all systems
VP 2025-08-07 3:19 p.m.
You could do this as a hack - but not ideal: def set_disconnected_callback(self, callback): """Alias for set_disconnect_callback to maintain compatibility.""" self.set_disconnect_callback(callback) Jokes aside -- here is an actual fix for that: % python test_cli_fix.py Connecting to MeshCore device with address: 1D101833-ECE0-6122-257A-59CAD52589B6 DEBUG:meshcore:Connecting with client: None, address: 1D101833-ECE0-6122-257A-59CAD52589B6, device: None INFO:meshcore:Scanning for devices... INFO:meshcore:Found device: 1D101833-ECE0-6122-257A-59CAD52589B6: MeshCore-58410B4F INFO:meshcore:BLE Connection started DEBUG:meshcore:Connected successfully: 1D101833-ECE0-6122-257A-59CAD52589B6 DEBUG:meshcore:Sending appstart command DEBUG:meshcore:Sending raw data: bytearray(b'\x01\x03 mccli') DEBUG:meshcore:Dispatching event: EventType.CONNECTED, {'connection_info': '1D101833-ECE0-6122-257A-59CAD52589B6'}, {} DEBUG:meshcore:Waiting for events [<EventType.SELF_INFO: 'self_info'>], timeout=5.0 DEBUG:meshcore:Received data: 0501161658410b4f67127b9700ca58e315ef18d26339f7deb7a699c02e74f87390a81bfc000000000000000000000000bde40d0090d003000b053538343130423446 DEBUG:meshcore:Dispatching event: EventType.SELF_INFO, {'adv_type': 1, 'tx_power': 22, 'max_tx_power': 22, 'public_key': '58410b4f67127b9700ca58e315ef18d26339f7deb7a699c02e74f87390a81bfc', 'adv_lat': 0.0, 'adv_lon': 0.0, 'adv_loc_policy': 0, 'telemetry_mode_env': 0, 'telemetry_mode_loc': 0, 'telemetry_mode_base': 0, 'manual_add_contacts': False, 'radio_freq': 910.525, 'radio_bw': 250.0, 'radio_sf': 11, 'radio_cr': 5, 'name': '58410B4F'}, {} Successfully connected to 58410B4F Device Information: Name: 58410B4F Public Key: 58410b4f67127b9700ca58e315ef18d26339f7deb7a699c02e74f87390a81bfc DEBUG:meshcore:BLE device disconnected: 1D101833-ECE0-6122-257A-59CAD52589B6 (is_connected: False) DEBUG:meshcore:BLE Connection closed Disconnected successfully

Get channel info 2. Set channel 3. Exit Enter choice (1-3): 1 Enter channel index (0-255): 0 Getting info for channel 0... 10:50 a.m.

FDLamotte 2025-09-06 11:53 a.m.
Just released meshcore-py v2.1.6,
  • binary commands have been made asynchronous
  • new methods send_msg_with_retry which retries if msg is not acked and fallbacks to flood for a last try

1
1
1
11:54 a.m.
a release of meshcore-cli supporting latest features has been released too, 1.1.20
VP 2025-09-06 2:17 p.m.
@FDLamotte thats awesome!!
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-10-12 10:03 p.m.
Hey, I've been building a couple of projects on meshcore_py and it has been great. I just submitted a PR to add support for the firmware's private key export—we're using the device's private key to sign JWTs for the new version of meshcoretomqtt and adding companion support, so extracting it was kind of necessary. I'll submit a separate PR later to add the device signing functions too, but I suspect that will be slightly less straightforward.
1
FDLamotte 2025-10-13 1:34 p.m.
v1.1.26 is out with better channel support ... big drawback is loading time in chat mode on ble (we need to find how to speedup channel info retrieval !)
Cisien 2025-10-13 2:57 p.m.
Remove the arbitrary delay?
awol 2025-10-13 3:43 p.m.
what is the improved channel support?
awol
what is the improved channel support?
FDLamotte 2025-10-14 12:52 a.m.
a get_channels command in cli that lists channels and also channels names can now be used instead of numbers (and are displayed when receiving msg)

Theo 2025-10-18 11:04 p.m.
Hey guys, just wondering if I'm doing something wrong with meshcli? If I enter interactive mode I can see my companion radio contacts, but if I try to do anything regarding contacts without being in interactive mode I am just told there are 0 contacts in device. This applies to contacts, list, rc and req_status
Theo 2025-10-19 12:35 a.m.
UPDATE: I flashed my companion radio to serial FW instead of Bluetooth and contacts have worked immediately. I suspect that the connection may be closing before all the contacts are finished being sent via bluetooth as the debug flag shows quite a bit of data being transferred before I end up with the "0 contacts on device" issue.
Theo 2025-10-19 4:13 a.m.
Just following up regarding the chat we had in the development channel, I was looking for a workaround to rebooting my companion radio to resync the serial. Unfortunately uhubctl doesn't do the trick, but neither does meshcli -s /dev/ttyUSB0 reboot
4:14 a.m.
meshcli -s /dev/ttyUSB0 -D reboot DEBUGport opened INFOSerial Connection started DEBUGConnected successfully: /dev/ttyUSB0 DEBUGSending appstart command DEBUGSending raw data: bytearray(b'\x01\x03 mccli') DEBUGsending pkt : b'<\r\x00\x01\x03 mccli' DEBUGWaiting for events [<EventType.SELF_INFO: 'self_info'>], timeout=5.0 DEBUGDispatching event: EventType.CONNECTED, {'connection_info': '/dev/ttyUSB0'}, {} DEBUGReceived data: 01032020202020206d63636c69 DEBUGDispatching event: EventType.ERROR, {'error_code': 3}, {} DEBUGSending device query command DEBUGSending raw data: 1603 DEBUGsending pkt : b'<\x02\x00\x16\x03' DEBUGWaiting for events [<EventType.DEVICE_INFO: 'device_info'>, <EventType.ERROR: 'command_error'>], timeout=5.0 ERRORError while querying device: Event(type=<EventType.ERROR: 'command_error'>, payload={'reason': 'no_event_received'}, attributes={})
FDLamotte 2025-10-19 4:38 a.m.
yes because the reboot command won't make it to the node
4:39 a.m.
don't know why I never experienced this on my raspberry pi ...

FDLamotte 2025-10-19 4:40 a.m.

Garth (VK2TTY)
G'day! How do we get our node's public key via the API? Get its private key, if permitted, and then extract the public key? Something else?
FDLamotte 2025-10-19 4:40 a.m.
the public key is in node infos
1
4:43 a.m.
so in python, you should get it (after an appstart) by reading mc.self_info['public_key']
1
4:44 a.m.
for the private key, there is an example (it has just been pulled)
4:44 a.m.

Theo
of course haha, thanks for your patience fdlamotte, as you can probably tell... I'm quite new to this
FDLamotte 2025-10-19 12:54 p.m.
catched the error, it was very incidious ... I am not waiting for the event telling me the contact were here ! if you do mccli lc sleep 1 lc the second lc shows you the contact fetched by the first lc it must have been this way for a long time ...
1
FDLamotte 2025-10-19 1:11 p.m.
I'll now wait for the CONTACTS event in ensure_contacts to me this should always have been like this ... to get it asyncronously one can still call get_contacts @awol just to keep you informed 'cause I'll push it that way ...
FDLamotte
I'll now wait for the CONTACTS event in ensure_contacts to me this should always have been like this ... to get it asyncronously one can still call get_contacts @awol just to keep you informed 'cause I'll push it that way ...
FDLamotte 2025-10-20 10:26 a.m.
@awol seems my fix was a huge mistake, because get_contacts already waited for CONTACTS event ... the issue might be with timeout too short (now contacts take 6s to fetch through bluetooth), and if there is a timeout contacts are not passed to subsequent command (if it does not wait) ... so I'll just increase timeout in get_contacts ... (to 30s !)
10:28 a.m.
thats it ... it was just a misadjusted timeout
10:29 a.m.
let's clean the mess now ...
awol 2025-10-20 10:51 a.m.
There are a lot of contacts these days (edited)

FDLamotte 2025-10-22 4:27 a.m.
4:27 a.m.
now showing progress when fetching contacts and channels
1
4:29 a.m.
@awol I had to refactor a little the send method in base.py (dispatcher.wait_for_event could only waited for one event so I used the code from commands/base.py)
taedryn 2025-10-22 2:43 p.m.
i'm running into a problem using meshcore_py, where i have a BBS which seems to be responding too quickly to incoming packets. the setup i have is using a BLE companion to connect to the BBS, which is running a USB companion hooked up to a computer running the BBS code. when i send a command to the BBS, it replies instantaneously, before my phone client even thinks its message has been ack'd. then the BBS sends the same thing a couple more times, because it times out waiting for the ack from the client. i suspect that the initial command response is overriding the ack response from the BBS's node. is there any way to see, in the python library, the current state of a sent message, in terms of whether the ack has been sent? is this a situation where it makes more sense to just sleep for a bit before leaping into action?
FDLamotte 2025-10-22 3:20 p.m.
I don't remember if we implemented automatic async ack tracking, I think @awol could respond to this What I currently do in mccli (as I have a user in front) is that I track last message sent for the ack, the ack event can be filtered using the expected_ack tag set by the companion radio The function that does it is in meshcore_py (it's send_msg_with_retry in src/meshcore/commands/messaging.py to track acks from multiple messages, the idea would be to have a table with expected_ack and an expected time (calculated from expected_timeout) and have some process wait for the acks and notifying you that the message has been acked. Don't know if it is necessary as one usually don't have more than one message out at a time (due to the characteristics of lora)
taedryn 2025-10-22 5:31 p.m.
ooh, expected_timeout is good to know about. i need to dig into this further. i was able to solve my too-quick response by brute-forcing a 500 ms delay before responding to a command, but that certainly doesn't scale if the connection is indirect. i'm using send_msg_with_retry, and that will be very helpful as it's likely the bbs will be launching messages to several different people at once during peak usage.
5:32 p.m.
i am keeping track of expected_ack reception, and using that as the basis for my timeout logic
FDLamotte
@awol I had to refactor a little the send method in base.py (dispatcher.wait_for_event could only waited for one event so I used the code from commands/base.py)
FDLamotte 2025-10-22 5:47 p.m.
think I'm missing CONTACTS event when it comes at the same time as NEXT_CONTACT ... so I've inlined the wait logic in get_contacts to keep waiting for contacts ... seems to work better (had issues with serial and wifi which goes too fast)
FDLamotte 2025-10-25 2:16 p.m.
tried to find a synthetic way of showing traces
2:17 p.m.
but I watch them on packet analyzer just use mccli to launch them ...
FDLamotte 2025-10-25 4:09 p.m.
with some colors

Cisien 2025-10-26 12:20 p.m.

12:20 p.m.

12:20 p.m.

Cisien 2025-10-26 12:21 p.m.
Ok, its not my idea then

taedryn

Cisien 2025-10-26 12:21 p.m.

taedryn 2025-10-26 12:21 p.m.

taedryn
I may turn auto add back on and experiment with my local node. What's your idea?
Cisien 2025-10-26 12:22 p.m.
Old date causing the advert to be ignored
taedryn 2025-10-26 12:22 p.m.

12:22 p.m.
So, my local nodes all see the adverts from my problem node. Just the BBS node doesn't
12:24 p.m.
Actually, that's incorrect. My local nodes all saw mcarper's advert though, which is the other problem one
12:26 p.m.
His date was reasonably current. Within a few minutes for sure.
12:27 p.m.
Someone reported on my BBS thread that they couldn't get a node name with an @ in the name to be recognized. My problem node doesn't have that situation, nor does mcarper's, but that was interesting to see.
taedryn

1:32 p.m.

FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:44 p.m.
I have some combination of nodes that won't hear advert from the other (my t114 won't hear adverts from my heltec-ct62, but accepts it if another node resends it ...) I sometimes don't receive advert events, and it's generally because the receiver already has received more recent packets from the advertizer (so it is discarded). In this case there should be something in the serial logs (if activated). You can either synchronize the advertizer or reset the receiver
taedryn 2025-10-26 1:46 p.m.
the situation i'm running into is that the receiver doesn't have the sender's advert stored. there's no possibility of receiving DMs in that scenario. is it any packets from the transmitter, or specifically DM packets that will cause the receiver to discard an advert?
1:47 p.m.
and is this in the node firmware, or in the python layer?
1:47 p.m.
i assume it's in the firmware
1
FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:47 p.m.
it might not have the advert stored but already received a packet (edited)
taedryn
i assume it's in the firmware
FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:47 p.m.
yes, in the firmware
taedryn 2025-10-26 1:48 p.m.
so, if the sender sends a couple channel messages, then an advert, that advert might be discarded by the receiver, even though the receiver doesn't have a current pubkey stored for the sender?
FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:48 p.m.
you can experience it by removing a contact from receiver, reseting the emiter, and make it send an advert (from mccli, which does not automatically sync)
taedryn
so, if the sender sends a couple channel messages, then an advert, that advert might be discarded by the receiver, even though the receiver doesn't have a current pubkey stored for the sender?
FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:48 p.m.
yes,
1:49 p.m.
if the emiter is reset
1:49 p.m.
and not synced

taedryn 2025-10-26 1:49 p.m.
hmm. that seems like a flawed design
FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:49 p.m.
it to prevent replays
taedryn 2025-10-26 1:49 p.m.

FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:49 p.m.

taedryn
is it a timestamp problem? like, the receiver has packets from 2 minutes ago, so an advert timestamped 5 minutes ago is discarded?
FDLamotte 2025-10-26 1:50 p.m.
yes
taedryn 2025-10-26 1:50 p.m.

time is not that critical, nodes can drift and it's generally not a problem

Is there a block like on a repeater for updating the time if it is already set? 2025-10-27 10:09:07 - PacketCapture - INFO - ✓ get_time works! Current device time: 1761584965 2025-10-27 10:09:07 - PacketCapture - INFO - Attempting to set radio clock to: 1761584947 2025-10-27 10:09:07 - PacketCapture - WARNING - Failed to set radio clock - Error code: 6
Cisien 2025-10-27 1:22 p.m.
if get_time returns the time on the device, then it looks like you're trying to set a time that's in the past
taedryn 2025-10-27 1:48 p.m.
is this a case where you need to set time directly after a reboot?
1:49 p.m.
like, so the node doesn't get unhappy about moving time backwards?
awol 2025-10-27 2:04 p.m.

2:04 p.m.

taedryn 2025-10-27 2:10 p.m.
yes please
1
Cisien
if get_time returns the time on the device, then it looks like you're trying to set a time that's in the past
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-10-27 2:20 p.m.
Yeah, that was my expectation. I guess setting the time could just be something that fails unless it is needed.
Cisien 2025-10-27 4:19 p.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I'm struggling to get set_time() to work on my test Heltec V3. I'm calling self.meshcore.commands.set_time(current_timestamp) where current_timestamp = int(time.time()), and getting an error. Is there a block like on a repeater for updating the time if it is already set? 2025-10-27 10:09:07 - PacketCapture - INFO - ✓ get_time works! Current device time: 1761584965 2025-10-27 10:09:07 - PacketCapture - INFO - Attempting to set radio clock to: 1761584947 2025-10-27 10:09:07 - PacketCapture - WARNING - Failed to set radio clock - Error code: 6
FDLamotte 2025-10-27 4:19 p.m.
you can't set time back ... and it even raises the error if you give the current time
FDLamotte
you can't set time back ... and it even raises the error if you give the current time
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-10-27 4:35 p.m.
Okay. I'll just compare device time to current before deciding whether to try to set the clock. Makes me wonder if my MQTT uplink fork that includes NTP is going to throw errors if device time drifts forward.
Cisien 2025-10-27 4:36 p.m.
or just swallow the error
4:36 p.m.
or warn if it's significantly too far forward
Cisien
or warn if it's significantly too far forward
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-10-27 4:38 p.m.
Yeah. Right now it only tries to set the time once when it boots, so it's probably fine. If I ever added a check every 24 hours it would fail on time drift.

the error is not an issue ...

Theo 2025-10-30 3:14 a.m.
I've found an issue getting neighbors from a repeater: If I connect to my local repeater (0 hops) in chat mode and request the neighbors with the following commands:
  • meshcli -s COM21 chat
  • login <repeater name> <password>
  • to <repeater name>
  • neighbors This returns me: "[s[A[999D[S[L[0;35mHAL-RPT [0;38;5;214m(D)[0;38;5;247m: 900E458C:541:-28[u[s[A[999D[S[L6B282EFD:1192:-20[u[s[A[999D[S[LDA8F17EF:1527:25[u[s[A[999D[S[LD453FCA8:33898:-25[u[s[A[999D[S[L1F67A704:61674:-9[u" After getting a return like this my repeater begins misbehaving and/or not responding to meshcli. The only solution I have found so far it to log in to my repeater from the meshcore app and reboot from there.
3:16 a.m.
I've tried this a few times and on more than one occasion I've seen messages which the repeater had received returned in plain text on the output of the neighbors command:
FDLamotte 2025-10-30 4:41 a.m.
it's a display issue, latest version makes space for neighbors output (which is multiline) so you should have something like that :
4:43 a.m.
as your terminal does not seem to support the characters I use, you should probably use classic_prompt
4:43 a.m.
which you can enable in your init file (in ~/.config/meshcore dir)
4:44 a.m.
you can even disable colors, but it seems your terminal supports it
4:45 a.m.
4:46 a.m.
I don't understand how it can put repeater in a bad state as this is just a cli command (like other cli commands) so there might be something wrong in repeater firmware ...
4:47 a.m.
I saw a bug as been discovered that caused a reboot .. maybe related ?

Theo

FDLamotte 2025-10-30 8:44 a.m.

1
FDLamotte 2025-10-30 8:52 a.m.
I'll have to test with a serial companion then
8:55 a.m.
is your serial companion from flasher ? Or have you built it ? I ask because if you built it and added logging via serial (at the same time as usb companion) you can have issues (as mccli will interpret all data (including logs) as from companion protocol)
9:02 a.m.
also, one thing to know is mccli works on windows by chance, it should not even support the serial companions because the lib I use for that (pyserial-asyncio) is not supposed to work on windows .. you might have more chances using WSL (if you're not already doing that)
9:02 a.m.
I'll ask a colleague that uses mccli on windows if he has issues using serial companions
Theo 2025-10-30 9:11 a.m.
Yep my companion was flashed just today via the Web flasher, not a custom build
9:12 a.m.
Oh! That's good to know, I'll try it with a Linux environment tomorrow instead of windows

my buddy told me mccli works really bad with serial companions on windows

FDLamotte 2025-11-07 2:08 p.m.
flood_scope management is implemented in latest mccli (from 1.2.6) through the scope command
2:08 p.m.
in 1.2.8, when in chat mode, it is possible to set the scope for one command only
2:09 p.m.
if you want to login but don't want the flood login to go too far, you can restrict it by doing login%#region
2:10 p.m.
% is not the best character, but it's one that I encountered in no node name (searching in letsme.sh)
2:10 p.m.
similarly you can restrict the flood for one message doing /Public%#region msg (edited)
2:11 p.m.
you can also combine to and scope in one command doing to dest%#scope
2:12 p.m.
if you are restricted to a region and want to send a broader message /Public% msg as the empty string is equivalent to * (edited)
2:14 p.m.
I'll probably try to document scope management in ?scope and ?to

FDLamotte 2025-11-08 2:26 a.m.

2:26 a.m.
you can put it in your init file
2:27 a.m.

2:28 a.m.
there is another arrow, but it does not take the whole space in height
2:28 a.m.

henesy [pnw] 2025-11-09 1:35 a.m.

1:35 a.m.

1:35 a.m.
Specifically for non interactive calls
henesy [pnw] 2025-11-09 1:48 a.m.

1:49 a.m.
also: is BLE on windows always kind of flaky? I get INFO:meshcore:Couldn't find device for probably ⅕ calls even though i'm paired and nothing special seems to happen when a call succeeds without the error
henesy [pnw]
Is there any way to set the cli to not send color codes?
FDLamotte 2025-11-09 3:05 a.m.
set color off should disable most color outputs (edited)
3:05 a.m.
except for traces
henesy [pnw] 2025-11-09 3:06 a.m.
What's the flag for this? I'm looking under -h
FDLamotte 2025-11-09 3:07 a.m.
it's a command not a flag
3:07 a.m.
can be put in your init file or before issuing other commands
3:08 a.m.
for non-interactive you ca get json output with -j
3:08 a.m.
I might add a flag for color
henesy [pnw] 2025-11-09 3:09 a.m.
Oh I see
3:09 a.m.
I didn't look at the config setting a
3:09 a.m.
Flag would be nice
3:10 a.m.
I thought I got color still even with json for some reason but it might have just been tty corruption
FDLamotte 2025-11-09 3:11 a.m.
in interactive mode yes you'll have color for the prompt
FDLamotte 2025-11-09 5:43 a.m.
@henesy [pnw] did a quick release (1.2.11) with -c off option to turn off color, there should now also be no color in traces and prompt won't display in bold when color=off ...

Thomas 2025-11-09 2:30 p.m.
whats the difference between "/set" and "set"
FDLamotte 2025-11-09 2:30 p.m.
/ is to say you send the command on the device ...
2:30 p.m.
so at root, they are equivalent
2:31 p.m.
but when you're in a device using to then set is applied to the device
Thomas 2025-11-09 2:31 p.m.
ah i see (edited)
FDLamotte 2025-11-09 2:32 p.m.
that's new in 1.2 and need to be documented
2:32 p.m.
but you can also directly enter a command for a device from anywhere doing /device/command
2:33 p.m.
and send a message to a channel or contact doing /channel message or /device message

henesy [pnw] 2025-11-09 7:32 p.m.
ok, serial doesn't connect to the COMx port for me in windows, BUT under WSL1 win10 i can use /dev/ttySx to connect to a serial companion - referenced: https://technofossy.com/how-to-access-serial-ports-com-ttys-in-windows-subsystem-for-linux-wsl1-wsl2/
M29 2025-11-10 3:07 p.m.
is it possible to purge contacts within the Cli? Total beginner to all this so please be gentle!
henesy [pnw] 2025-11-10 4:39 p.m.
you can do remove_contact
4:41 p.m.
maybe less useful interactively, but you can do contacts with -j and then script removing contacts using the field "last_advert"
4:42 p.m.
it would be nice to have a prune_contacts <hours> command that handled that logic internally
FDLamotte 2025-11-11 2:16 a.m.
I've implemented an apply_to not so long ago (can be shorten with at), that can do what you want with prune_contact And I've even provided an help ?at apply_to <scope> <cmd> : applies cmd to contacts matching scope Scope acts like a filter with comma separated fields : - u, matches modification time < or > than a timestamp (can also be days hours or minutes ago if followed by d,h or m) - t, matches the type (1: client, 2: repeater, 3: room, 4: sensor) - h, matches number of hops - d, direct, similar to h>-1 - f, flood, similar to h<0 or h=-1 Note: Some commands like contact_name (aka cn), reset_path (aka rp), forget_password (aka fp) can be chained. Examples: # removes all clients that have not been updated in last 2 days at u<2d,t=1 remove_contact # gives traces to repeaters that have been updated in the last 24h and are direct at t=2,u>1d,d cn trace # tries to do flood login to all repeaters at t=2 rp login
2:16 a.m.
this can be used from bash, but I advise to put commas around some fields
1
2:17 a.m.
it's also interesting to verify which contacts will be deleted, before, for that I would issue mccli at "u<7d" "contact_lastmod"
2:21 a.m.
to remove all contacts ... at all remove_contact
henesy [pnw] 2025-11-11 2:26 a.m.
Very cool!
FDLamotte
it's also interesting to verify which contacts will be deleted, before, for that I would issue mccli at "u<7d" "contact_lastmod"

I've added some fields to json_rx_log doesn't go in payload yet, but header, path, type are there ... {"raw_hex": "31e20200251d9810ef3edad144c4c3a41d43863ded94e27f", "snr": 12.25, "rssi": -30, "payload": "0200251d9810ef3edad144c4c3a41d43863ded94e27f", "payload_length": 22, "header": 2, "route_type": 2, "payload_type": 0, "payload_typename": "REQ", "payload_ver": 0, "path_len": 0, "path": "", "pkt_payload": "251d9810ef3edad144c4c3a41d43863ded94e27f"} {"raw_hex": "31cf05001d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407", "snr": 12.25, "rssi": -49, "payload": "05001d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407", "payload_length": 22, "header": 5, "route_type": 1, "payload_type": 1, "payload_typename": "RESPONSE", "payload_ver": 0, "path_len": 0, "path": "", "pkt_payload": "1d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407"} {"raw_hex": "35fc0501231d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407", "snr": 13.25, "rssi": -4, "payload": "0501231d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407", "payload_length": 23, "header": 5, "route_type": 1, "payload_type": 1, "payload_typename": "RESPONSE", "payload_ver": 0, "path_len": 1, "path": "23", "pkt_payload": "1d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407"} {"raw_hex": "37de0501bb1d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407", "snr": 13.75, "rssi": -34, "payload": "0501bb1d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407", "payload_length": 23, "header": 5, "route_type": 1, "payload_type": 1, "payload_typename": "RESPONSE", "payload_ver": 0, "path_len": 1, "path": "bb", "pkt_payload": "1d256d99a0f04efe2d9c0da0cdfaf4d918e9b407"}

taedryn 2025-11-14 11:07 a.m.
What was the bug in node discovery? I've been having trouble with using the library to get all contacts for the BBS, but it's been pretty intermittent. I'll have to try the new version and see if it changes anything.
FDLamotte 2025-11-14 11:31 a.m.
just that I was using get_contact_by_key and not checking if None before using it ... but that's in meshcore-cli
11:31 a.m.
node_discovery is to find nodes that are 0-hop, only repeaters and sensors have the code to respond atm
taedryn 2025-11-14 1:36 p.m.
ah, ok
1:39 p.m.
looks like the problem i was having was that sometimes, apparently at random, meshcore.commands.get_contacts() would return None. i've got checks in place to deal with it now, but it was odd because it would only happen fairly rarely without any obvious pattern. i use the function all the time, and it was probably only 1/100 of the time that it would return None. (edited)
1:43 p.m.
yeah. 12/937 calls to it failed over the last two weeks of running the bbs on the rpi. i assume it's some kind of transient communication error with the node (which is connected over USB)

FDLamotte 2025-11-15 10:08 a.m.

taedryn 2025-11-15 1:49 p.m.
i'm seeing the following error popping up, and i'm working on catching it properly, but @FDLamotte any theories what might be happening? [2025-11-15 15:57:09] ERROR asyncio: Task exception was never retrieved future: <Task finished name='Task-20481' coro=<MessageReader.handle_rx() done, defined at /home/citadel/mesh-citadel/.mesh-citadel/lib/python3.13/site-packages/meshcore/reader.py:50> exception=IndexError('index out of range')> Traceback (most recent call last): File "/home/citadel/mesh-citadel/.mesh-citadel/lib/python3.13/site-packages/meshcore/reader.py", line 51, in handle_rx packet_type_value = data[0] ~~~~^^^ IndexError: index out of range my guess is that my companion firmware is doing something silly. this is an ESP32 wio xiao usb companion that's left up for long periods of time, and i suspect is becoming unreliable as time goes on, maybe with a memory leak or something.
FDLamotte 2025-11-15 1:56 p.m.
you seem to have received an empty packet
1:56 p.m.
but I'm puzled because my reader.py doesn't have that at line 51 ...
1:57 p.m.
and this line has been changed to packet_type_value = dbuf.read(1)[0] at line 54 (edited)
1:59 p.m.
which will probably result to the same error ...
1:59 p.m.
but you should probably update the lib
taedryn 2025-11-15 2:02 p.m.
yes, true. my companion is being weird, now i can't communicate with it at all after updating to 1.10. probably not a library problem
taedryn 2025-11-15 6:07 p.m.
my node seem to regularly go into a state where it delivers a long series of empty packets to the library, which croaks with the index error. it usually takes most of a day connecting from the bbs before i get the error, though, so it's hard to track down.
FDLamotte
and this line has been changed to packet_type_value = dbuf.read(1)[0] at line 54 (edited)
taedryn 2025-11-15 6:20 p.m.
what do you think about starting that function out like this instead? async def handle_rx(self, data: bytearray): dbuf = io.BytesIO(data) try: packet_type_value = dbuf.read(1)[0] except IndexError as e: logger.warning(f"Received empty packet: {e}") return logger.debug(f"Received data: {data.hex()}")
6:21 p.m.
at least in my case, getting that IndexError seems to indicate an internal fault in the node, as it stops receiving at that point, which is why i suggest logger.warning there
6:23 p.m.
but it might make as much sense to leave it as it is, although it's effectively an uncatchable exception, because the event which results in handle_rx() doesn't seem to be exposed to the calling function using meshcore.start_auto_message_fetching()
taedryn
what do you think about starting that function out like this instead? async def handle_rx(self, data: bytearray): dbuf = io.BytesIO(data) try: packet_type_value = dbuf.read(1)[0] except IndexError as e: logger.warning(f"Received empty packet: {e}") return logger.debug(f"Received data: {data.hex()}")
FDLamotte 2025-11-16 10:44 a.m.
it's sound, I'll do it ...
FDLamotte 2025-11-16 11:23 a.m.
in git for the moment, will probably released with some incoming PR when this is ready
1
taedryn 2025-11-16 12:26 p.m.
Thanks!
dt267 2025-11-16 11:36 p.m.
Hello, I got this error today. Connection lost: ble_disconnect when using login. Update: It worked when I synced the clock on both devices. (edited)
taedryn 2025-11-17 2:48 p.m.
so i'm seeing this series of messages, and it finally occurred to me to look at them more carefully: [2025-11-17 19:28:13] INFO meshcore: Resetting path [2025-11-17 19:28:13] ERROR meshcore: Couldn't reset path Event(type=<EventType.ERROR: 'command_error'>, payload={'error_code': 1}, attributes={}) continuing ...
2:49 p.m.
is this telling me that it is unable to switch to flood routing? this is in the middle of retrying a message send operation using send_message_with_retry()
taedryn 2025-11-17 3:22 p.m.
Yes, that's what this error indicates. What does error code 1 mean? Where can I look that up?
taedryn 2025-11-17 7:23 p.m.
i see that reset_path() is sending 0x0d like i'd expect.
taedryn 2025-11-17 10:52 p.m.
I ended up making this: https://github.com/taedryn/mesh-citadel/blob/main/docs/MeshCore_Error_Codes.md
mesh-citadel/docs/MeshCore_Error_Codes.md at main · taedryn/mesh-c...
A MeshCore-first BBS, inspired by the Citadel BBSes of the 80s - taedryn/mesh-citadel
FDLamotte 2025-11-18 5:17 a.m.
@taedryn I first thought it might happen when path is already flood, but no my next guess is that you sent your message using pubkey_prefix ? this should be ok but might not work when reseting path, because the key must be 32bytes for this call I think I should call reset_path on the contact if it exists (or not reset if not as we don't know and might not want to get all the contacts)
5:19 a.m.
I think we should raise a ValueError in _validate_destination if the dst length is inferior to prefix_len ...
FDLamotte 2025-11-18 5:28 a.m.
@taedryn I've pushed a change in git (checking for the good key size)
5:29 a.m.
it should break reset_contact if called with a key_prefix too small (edited)
5:29 a.m.
then I'll try to change send_msg_with_retry using contact as parameter to reset_path
FDLamotte
I think we should raise a ValueError in _validate_destination if the dst length is inferior to prefix_len ...
taedryn 2025-11-18 11:04 a.m.
Interestingly, that's exactly the fix I was playing with last night and was going to suggest. Thanks for working on it! At least for my quick test example the send function was indeed passing too short a value to reset_path.
FDLamotte 2025-11-18 11:06 a.m.
ok I'll work on send_message_with_retry then, so that it check if it can easily access full key (from contact if it could get it)
taedryn 2025-11-18 11:06 a.m.
Perfect, I think that's exactly the right solution.
FDLamotte 2025-11-18 11:06 a.m.
you can pass contact to reset_path for now as a fix
taedryn 2025-11-18 11:09 a.m.
Thanks, I'm going to update my code this evening.
11:10 a.m.
(I'm in UTC-8, so we have wildly different "evening" times since I think you're in UTC+1)
FDLamotte 2025-11-18 11:13 a.m.
yes meshcore dev never stops

Peetz0r 2025-12-07 2:19 a.m.
G'morning! I noticed print_new_contacts gets reset very often
2:19 a.m.
and I think this line is to blame: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-cli/blob/main/src/meshcore_cli/meshcore_cli.py#L862
meshcore-cli/src/meshcore_cli/meshcore_cli.py at main · meshcore-d...
Command line interface to MeshCore node. Contribute to meshcore-dev/meshcore-cli development by creating an account on GitHub.
2:20 a.m.
but I also wondered why every time I do /to / or similar, it starts an entire new "interactive session"
FDLamotte 2025-12-07 2:27 a.m.
yes, each time you start a new interactive session it resets print_new_contacts but you're not supposed to start new interactive sessions (this is a side effect as to command from cmdline or giving the name of a node starts a session with this node)
2:27 a.m.
to should be called without a /
2:29 a.m.
it's funny you've ran into this (and it was kept intentionally because it's a funny behavior)
2:32 a.m.
I think I'll change behaviour of /to to act as to I'll also remove the setting of print_new_contacts
2:38 a.m.
now in git
Peetz0r 2025-12-07 3:15 a.m.
ooooh there's an actual difference between to and /to
3:17 a.m.
At first I expected everything without a / in front in a chat node to be sent out as text messages and a / in front of every command (edited)
3:17 a.m.
so when I learned that you can do to in a chat node I expected to just be a shorthand for /to
3:20 a.m.
after re-reading the interactive mode section in the readme, I think I understand it better now

another feature request: saving preferences such as print_new_contacts

Peetz0r
another feature request: saving preferences such as print_new_contacts
FDLamotte 2025-12-07 4:04 a.m.

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-16 8:56 a.m.
I sent over a PR to implement the on-device signing commands in meshcore_py. Let me know if there's anything you would like changed. I can roll it all up in one commit if you'd prefer. This is going to make meshcoretomqtt and meshcore-packet-capture so much easier for people to use.
2
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I sent over a PR to implement the on-device signing commands in meshcore_py. Let me know if there's anything you would like changed. I can roll it all up in one commit if you'd prefer. This is going to make meshcoretomqtt and meshcore-packet-capture so much easier for people to use.
FDLamotte 2025-12-16 11:52 a.m.
let me have a look ... I'm a little slow at the moment
11:56 a.m.
oh, that's really cool ... was thinking just about that the other day, and it was already there (been in the firmware for 6 month ...)
11:59 a.m.
seems good as it is, I want to keep as close as possible to the companion protocol and you did it nicely,
11:59 a.m.
thanks !

avion 2025-12-16 2:55 p.m.

FDLamotte 2025-12-17 5:52 a.m.
@avion commands sent to repeaters are the ones supported by repeaters themselves ... (edited)
5:52 a.m.
get coords does not exist
5:53 a.m.
I've added it to the companion commands for meshcore-cli to set coordinate (with copy paste from mepo) with one command
5:54 a.m.
get lat and get lon are supported
5:54 a.m.
5:54 a.m.
that's what you should get
5:56 a.m.
if you query coords/lat/lon from the root, it gives you the coordinates of the companion
5:57 a.m.
you can send command to the repeater from root, using cmd command
5:57 a.m.
or the prefix (only in interactive mode) (edited)

FDLamotte 2025-12-18 9:34 a.m.
there is something not clear with the pin argument in bleak ... I've read a little about it and it does not seem to be meant to pass a pin, but to force asking the user for the pin but it does not seem consistent between platforms (edited)
9:35 a.m.
when I use the pin option (as a boolean) in meshcore-cli, I have bluetoothctl running at the same time
9:35 a.m.
it asks me there for the pin
9:37 a.m.
another current issue is with bleak 2.0, on my machines meshcore_py doesn't work with this version, it seems some events are sent before being awaited ! for the moment I force the use of older versions of bleak

Having real problems with meshcore 2.2.4 python library working with Heltec v3 on meshcore 11.1. Ble connects but then get error. Protocol error.. Python line... Mce=await Meshcore.create_ble("..hex", pin="234921") It never gets to next line.. Any ideas? John
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2025-12-18 11:03 p.m.
John, what platform are you on? When I was working on packet-capture, I had to write a whole thing to work around this issue. Basically, the pairing needs to happen prior to running your script. I ended up with this ugliness that generally gets the job done (although the install bash script that wraps around it basically shrugs and prints instructions on how to pair manually on Windows). https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-packet-capture/blob/main/ble_pairing_helper.py

Pasadena_Radio_User 2025-12-25 12:29 a.m.
Trying to get meshcore-cli (running on Debian) talking to my RAK4631 with v1.11 USB Comapnion installed. I have 2 versions of meshcore-cli installed and neither seem to work (v1.3.11 and v1.3.3) I was doing some searching on this topic and I see a similiar issue with BT devices but mine is serial. Here is meshcore-cli run with the debug flag: root@la-meshview:/home/slr/mc# /usr/local/bin/meshcore-cli -D -s /dev/ttyACM0 "" ver DEBUGport opened INFOSerial Connection started DEBUGConnected successfully: /dev/ttyACM0 DEBUGSending appstart command DEBUGSending raw data: bytearray(b'\x01\x03 mccli') DEBUGsending pkt : b'<\r\x00\x01\x03 mccli' DEBUGWaiting for events [<EventType.SELF_INFO: 'self_info'>], timeout=5.0 DEBUGDispatching event: EventType.CONNECTED, {'connection_info': '/dev/ttyACM0'}, {} DEBUGReceived data: 01032020202020206d63636c69 DEBUGDispatching event: EventType.ERROR, {'error_code': 3}, {} DEBUGSending device query command DEBUGSending raw data: 1603 DEBUGsending pkt : b'<\x02\x00\x16\x03' DEBUGWaiting for events [<EventType.DEVICE_INFO: 'device_info'>, <EventType.ERROR: 'command_error'>], timeout=5.0 ERRORError while querying device: Event(type=<EventType.ERROR: 'command_error'>, payload={'reason': 'no_event_received'}, attributes={}) root@la-meshview:/home/slr/mc#
12:30 a.m.
So it looks like I am getting a reply of some type: DEBUGReceived data: 01032020202020206d63636c69
12:32 a.m.
Any ideas ?
FDLamotte 2025-12-25 4:12 a.m.
In your case the connection seems to work as you get received data and an error code
4:12 a.m.
error code is 3 : ERR_CODE_TABLE_FULL
4:12 a.m.
codes are described here : https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/wiki/Companion-Radio-Protocol
4:16 a.m.
then you receive no event
4:16 a.m.
what I find strange is that reading the firmware, TABLE_FULL does not seem to be sent after an APP_START which is what happens here ... (edited)
4:21 a.m.
What version of meshcore_py is used ? I recently replied to https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore_py/issues/40 and changed the serial backend ...
pyserial-asyncio is deprecated, and will be blocked in Home Assista...
It should be replaced by pyserial-asyncio-fast: https://github.com/home-assistant-libs/pyserial-asyncio-fast See home-assistant/developers.home-assistant#2894 and home-assistant/core#116635
4:22 a.m.
maybe try to force the use of meshcore_py v2.2.3 or 2.2.4 as they still use the old backend
4:23 a.m.
won't be able to do much now, but I will have a look ...
Pasadena_Radio_User 2025-12-25 8:34 p.m.

FDLamotte 2025-12-27 4:26 a.m.
meshcore_py module name is meshcore, yes ...

FDLamotte 2025-12-28 4:57 a.m.
so my suggestion was to downgrade meshcore to 2.2.3 or 2.2.4 so pyserial-asyncio is used instead of pyserial-asyncio-fast ... the use of pyserial-asyncio-fast was introduced in 2.2.5 and while it works on my setups (and is said to be a dropin replacement) I'm not sure of it
FDLamotte
so my suggestion was to downgrade meshcore to 2.2.3 or 2.2.4 so pyserial-asyncio is used instead of pyserial-asyncio-fast ... the use of pyserial-asyncio-fast was introduced in 2.2.5 and while it works on my setups (and is said to be a dropin replacement) I'm not sure of it
Pasadena_Radio_User 2025-12-28 5:03 a.m.
I'll give that a shot - thank you.

pescheck-bram 2025-12-30 2:22 p.m.
While working on meshcore-cli I noted a few optional ideas and wanted to check first if any of these would be welcome. Please feel free to ignore if not. • Lightweight CI using ruff (lint and formatting only). Very fast and low noise, mainly to keep contributions consistent as the CLI grows. And possible to implement in actions like an other open source project of mine https://github.com/pescheckit/python-gpt-po • Optional Docker image for reproducible CLI runs or testing or user use. Could be handy for quick testing or avoiding local environment issues • Possibly splitting some of the Python code into smaller modules and making parts a bit more generic. Mainly to improve readability and make future changes easier, but only if that aligns with how you want to structure the project. • I also noticed there are argument parsing and autocomplete libraries available that could potentially simplify some CLI-related code. • Testing suite If any of these sound useful, I’m happy to prep small, incremental PRs so you can decide per item. (edited)
1
FDLamotte 2025-12-31 1:59 a.m.

MarWoj 2026-01-02 4:10 a.m.
How can I share my repeater on the meshcore map without the Meshcore mobile app but with meshcore-cli instead, provided I am logged in remotely to my repeater from a companion device?
MarWoj
How can I share my repeater on the meshcore map without the Meshcore mobile app but with meshcore-cli instead, provided I am logged in remotely to my repeater from a companion device?
pescheck-bram 2026-01-02 9:02 a.m.
meshcore-cli how i run this is i connected the repeater to a pi zero on other ocassions i connected it to a pi or computer. then you can run: meshcore-cli -r -s /dev/ttyACM0 Connecting to repeater at /dev/ttyACM0 (115200 baud)... Connected! Device: Repeater Type help for commands, quit to exit, Tab for completion -------------------------------------------------- Repeater> And then advert if i am not mistaken. And you can set the advert interval: set advert.interval 60. (edited)
pescheck-bram
meshcore-cli how i run this is i connected the repeater to a pi zero on other ocassions i connected it to a pi or computer. then you can run: meshcore-cli -r -s /dev/ttyACM0 Connecting to repeater at /dev/ttyACM0 (115200 baud)... Connected! Device: Repeater Type help for commands, quit to exit, Tab for completion -------------------------------------------------- Repeater> And then advert if i am not mistaken. And you can set the advert interval: set advert.interval 60. (edited)

FDLamotte 2026-02-04 8:31 a.m.
Just made a github release to acknoledge all the work from 1.3.2 (we're at 1.3.19) biggest addition has been repeater cli Thinking about releasing 1.4 soon, but I'd like to work on a tool for uploading regions in repeater mode https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-cli/releases/tag/v1.3.19
Release v1.3.19 · meshcore-dev/meshcore-cli
There has been lots of new features since 1.3.2 (there will probably be a 1.4 soon) get status command autoadd config anon requests (repeaters/owner/clock) repeater mode timestamps advert echoes

I am trying to get meshcore.py to work,,#!/usr/bin/python3.11 import asyncio import json from meshcore import MeshCore from meshcore import EventType from meshcore import SerialConnection async def main () : mc = await MeshCore.create_serial('COM7', 115200,debug=False) print("connected") await mc.start_auto_message_fetching() print("fetching") while True: result = await mc.wait_for_event(EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV) if result: #print(result) print(f"Hops: {result.payload.get('path_len')}", f"Chan: {result.payload.get('channel_idx')}" ) print(f"Msg:: {result.payload.get('text')}") asyncio.run(main())

FDLamotte 2026-02-09 8:09 a.m.

AstroJohn 2026-02-09 8:11 a.m.
I get Logs and messages but not adverts. Can send you current test python?
8:11 a.m.
#!/usr/bin/python3.11 import asyncio import json from meshcore import MeshCore from meshcore import EventType from meshcore import SerialConnection cnt = 0 async def main () : global cnt cnt = 0 mc = await MeshCore.create_serial('COM7', 115200,debug=True,auto_reconnect=True) print("connected") mc.subscribe(EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV, on_message) print("subscribed chan") mc.subscribe(EventType.CONTACT_MSG_RECV, on_contact) print("subscribed cont") mc.subscribe(EventType.ADVERTISEMENT, handle_advert) print("subscribed ad") mc.subscribe(EventType.RX_LOG_DATA, handle_log) print("subscribed log") mc.subscribe(EventType.DISCONNECTED, on_err) print("subscribed disc") await mc.start_auto_message_fetching() print("fetching") while True: await asyncio.sleep(0.01) if not mc.is_connected: print("Disconnected") async def on_err(event): global cnt print(f"No connect: {event.payload}") cnt = cnt+1 print(cnt) async def on_message(event): global cnt print("---") print(f"Chan: {event.payload['channel_idx']}", f"Hops: {event.payload['path_len']}" ) print(f"Msg: {event.payload['text']}") cnt = cnt+1 print(cnt) async def handle_advert(event): global cnt print(f"Ad: {event.payload}") cnt = cnt+1 print(cnt) async def handle_log(event): global cnt print(f"Log rssi: {event.payload['rssi']}",f" snr: {event.payload['snr']}",f" Payload: {event.payload['payload_length']}") cnt = cnt+1 print(cnt) async def on_contact(event): global cnt print("---") print(f"contact: {event.payload}") cnt = cnt+1 print(cnt) asyncio.run(main())
FDLamotte 2026-02-09 8:16 a.m.
I get that : 1 DBUG:meshcore:Received data: 8076290c80f5252290bdfb3218ce1234e912434e38c9fc2918e3491f212a296b17 DEBUG:meshcore:Advertisement received DEBUG:meshcore:Dispatching event: EventType.ADVERTISEMENT, {'public_key': '76290c80f5252290bdfb3218ce1234e912434e38c9fc2918e3491f212a296b17'}, {'public_key': '76290c80f5252290bdfb3218ce1234e912434e38c9fc2918e3491f212a296b17'} Ad: {'public_key': '76290c80f5252290bdfb3218ce1234e912434e38c9fc2918e3491f212a296b17'} 2 (edited)
8:19 a.m.
but if the node sending advert sends an advert with timestamp older than last advert, it will be discarded
8:19 a.m.
maybe that's what happens ?
1
8:20 a.m.
for example if you power up a repeater and try to get the initial advert, unless time is kept with rtc the advert won't show
8:21 a.m.

FDLamotte 2026-02-09 10:37 a.m.
maybe try to use the tcp companion ? or use a tool to transport serial over tcp (On linux I've been using socat for that with success) and on android tcpuart
10:42 a.m.
I've quickly found these tools ...
  • https://sourceforge.net/projects/com0com/ (see hub4com)
  • https://sourceforge.net/projects/tcpserialbridge/
 (edited)
Null-modem emulator
Download Null-modem emulator for free. The virtual serial port driver for Windows. The Null-modem emulator (com0com) is a kernel-mode virtual serial port driver for Windows. You can create an unlimited number of virtual COM port pairs and use any pair to connect one COM port based application to another.
TcpSerialBridge
Download TcpSerialBridge for free. Bridge From TCP to a COM Port. This is a handy utility to allow to connect to a serial port over TCP/IP. It can be used to allow a remote connection to a serial peripheral over the Internet.

FDLamotte 2026-02-09 11:58 a.m.
I'm amazed how much microsoft makes things difficult for using their OS ! just tried, and had driver issues, firewall issues and more issues to come
FDLamotte 2026-02-09 12:11 p.m.
I wanted to try on an esp32 device, but I have only thinknode M5 here and I think there must be an issue on the usb companion
12:12 p.m.
used a xiao-nrf52 usb companion, and from windows could use meshcore-cli directly (an receive channel messages)
12:15 p.m.
I also successfully used hub4com
  • got it from there https://sourceforge.net/projects/com0com/files/hub4com/2.1.0.0/
  • then used com2tcp.bat --baud 115200 COM6 5000 to create the uart/tcp bridge
  • and then meshcore-cli -t localhost to use the tcp connection
Null-modem emulator - Browse /hub4com/2.1.0.0 at SourceForge.net
The virtual serial port driver for Windows.
12:16 p.m.
connexion is much faster than directly connecting using serial
FDLamotte 2026-02-09 12:36 p.m.
ok, encoutered an heltecv3 in my boxes the one I use for espnow bridges
12:36 p.m.
and could do some tests
12:36 p.m.
your script works well
12:37 p.m.
things are better using the tcp connexion and a tcp/uart bridge, at least they are faster

ok, encoutered an heltecv3 in my boxes the one I use for espnow bridges

5:25 p.m.
